extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter waited until 350m from the finish to launch past GILBERT Philippe, beating KRISTOFF Alexander at the line with a bike throw for his third stage win.
tactical_patterns: GILBERT Philippe attacked solo when Quick-Step lost sprinter GAVIRIA Fernando, revealing they prioritize stage hunting over leadout discipline.
SAGAN Peter positioned on KRISTOFF Alexander's wheel with 400m remaining, using rivals' leadouts rather than his own team's work.
SAGAN Peter launched simultaneously with KRISTOFF Alexander at 300m, suggesting he matches rival sprint timing rather than initiating attacks.
FDJ provided full leadout for DÉMARE Arnaud despite mountain fatigue, indicating French teams overcommit domestiques even on recovery stages.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: won with deliberate 300m launch after three mountain stages, indicating superior recovery physiology compared to pure sprinters
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: neutral: matched SAGAN Peter's acceleration but lost on bike throw, revealing technical finishing weakness exploitable in future sprints
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: negative: caught with 350m remaining after solo move, suggesting insufficient power reserve when attacking from outside sprint trains
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: negative: received full FDJ leadout but failed to contest podium, indicating mountain stages degraded sprint speed more than rivals
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: maintained 1:39 gap over FROOME Chris without effort on transition stage, confirming yellow jersey security heading to Pyrenees
